art_style: Muted ink-wash realism blending Vietnamese folk woodcut aesthetics with spectral, dissolving edges, where figures and landscapes appear half-remembered and slowly fading into blank space.
texture: Coarse, damp mulberry paper showing faint salt crystallization and water damage at the margins.
mood: Melancholic, quiet, and impermanent, capturing the visual weight of slow erosion and things left untended.
reference_artists: Takahashi Maho, Tran Nguyen, Pham Huy Thong
cover_concept: A weathered wooden boat half-buried in pale, shifting sand, its hull facing a vast, empty sea under a colorless sky, with a single figure standing far in the distance, their edges blurring into the fog.
ornament_concept: Small, minimalist silhouettes of coastal flora bending in unseen winds, gradually dissolving into scattered dots or bare, skeletal lines.
scene_break_concept: A single, faint horizontal brushstroke of uneven thickness, resembling a tide line left on a shore after the water has receded.
map_concept: A delicate, incomplete ink chart of a shrinking coastline drawn on water-stained parchment, featuring subtle, dashed-line boundaries that physically fade into the blank ocean.
